title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: contributors maintainers project, pledge respect people contribute reporting issues, posting feature requests, updating documentation, submitting pull requests patches, activities. committed making participation project harassment-free experience everyone, regardless level experience, gender, gender identity expression, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, age, religion. Examples unacceptable behaviour participants include use sexual language imagery, derogatory comments personal attacks, trolling, public private harassment, insults, unprofessional conduct. Project maintainers right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct. Project maintainers follow Code Conduct may removed project team. Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behaviour may reported opening issue contacting one project maintainers. Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant (http://contributor-covenant.org), version 1.0.0, available http://contributor-covenant.org/version/1/0/0/

title: CONTRIBUTING
text: Small typos grammatical errors documentation may edited directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. E.g. edit roxygen2 comment .R file R/, .Rd file man/.

title: CONTRIBUTING
text: make substantial pull request, always file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s problem. ’ve found bug, create associated issue illustrate bug minimal reproducible example.

title: CONTRIBUTING
text: recommend create Git branch pull request (PR). Look GitHub Actions build status making changes. README contain badges continuous integration services used package. require tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2. use testthat. title: Authors and Citation
text: Thierry Onkelinx. Author, maintainer.            Research Institute Nature Forest (INBO) . Copyright holder, funder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Onkelinx, Thierry (2026) citeme: Manage Person Organisation Information. Version 0.1.0. https://github.com/inbo/citeme/
code: @Manual{,   title = {citeme: Manage Person and Organisation Information. Version 0.1.0},   author = {Thierry Onkelinx},   year = {2026},   url = {https://github.com/inbo/citeme/},   abstract = {Manage person and organisation information with validation and formatting capabilities. Provides R6 classes for managing organisations and their members, with support for multiple languages, ORCID identifiers, ROR identifiers, licensing requirements, and integration with citation management systems.},   keywords = {organisation; author; standardisation; inbo_version}, }

title: Manage Person and Organisation Information
text: Onkelinx, Thierry123 Research Institute Nature Forest (INBO)456 keywords: organisation, author, standardisation Manage person organisation information validation formatting capabilities. Provides R6 classes managing organisations members, support multiple languages, ORCID identifiers, ROR identifiers, licensing requirements, integration citation management systems.

title: Manage Person and Organisation Information
text: can install development version citeme GitHub :

title: Manage Person and Organisation Information
text: basic example shows create organisation item:
code: library(citeme)  # Create an organisation item org <- org_item$new(   name = c(     `en-GB` = "Research Institute for Nature and Forest (INBO)",     `nl-BE` = "Instituut voor Natuur- en Bosonderzoek (INBO)"   ),   email = "info@inbo.be",   orcid = TRUE,   rightsholder = "shared",   funder = "when no other" )  # Print the organisation org$print()

title: Manage Person and Organisation Information
text: Please note citeme project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

what: Ask for an ORCID iD — ask_orcid
title: Ask for an ORCID iD — ask_orcid
text: ORCID iD unique, persistent identifier free charge researchers. function prompts user enter ORCID iD validates format. ORCID iD must format 0000-0000-0000-0000 last digit can number "X". Empty strings considered valid allow optional ORCID iD.

title: Ask for an ORCID iD — ask_orcid
Ask for an ORCID iD — ask_orcid
text: prompt character string display prompt user. default "orcid: ".

title: Ask for an ORCID iD — ask_orcid
text: character string containing ORCID iD entered user.

title: Ask for an ROR — ask_ror
text: Research Organization Registry (ROR) global, community-led registry open persistent identifiers research organizations. ROR makes easy anyone system disambiguate institution names connect research organizations researchers research outputs.

text: prompt string printed prompting user input.   usually end space " ".

what: Cache organisation list from URL — cache_org
title: Cache organisation list from URL — cache_org

Cache organisation list from URL — cache_org
text: url URL organisation list retrieve. config_folder folder cached organisation list stored. Defaults user's R configuration directory citeme.

title: Cache organisation list from URL — cache_org
text: retrieved organisation list, NULL URL invalid repository.

what: Select the individual information to use as a person object — individual2person
title: Select the individual information to use as a person object — individual2person
text: function retrieves individual information using select_individual() converts person object. individual argument provided, prompt user select individual. person object include given name, family name, email, ORCID, affiliation (available) selected individual.

title: Select the individual information to use as a person object — individual2person
Select the individual information to use as a person object — individual2person
text: individual optional individual object convert person object. provided, function prompt user select individual. role role use person object. Defaults `"aut"“ (author). lang language use affiliation.

title: The org_item R6 class — org_item
code: org_item$new(   name,   email,   orcid = FALSE,   rightsholder = c("optional", "single", "shared", "when no other"),   funder = c("optional", "single", "shared", "when no other"),   license = list(package = c(`GPL-3.0` =     paste("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/inbo/checklist/refs/heads/main",     "inst/generic_template/gplv3.md", sep = "/"), MIT =     paste("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/inbo/checklist/refs/heads/main",     "inst/generic_template/mit.md", sep = "/")), project = c(`CC BY 4.0` =     paste("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/inbo/checklist/refs/heads/main",     "inst/generic_template/cc_by_4_0.md", sep = "/")), data = c(CC0 =     paste("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/inbo/checklist",     "131fe5829907079795533bfea767bf7df50c3cfd/inst/generic_template",      "cc0.md", sep     = "/"))),   ror = "",   zenodo = "",   website = "",   logo = "" )

title: The org_item R6 class — org_item
text: name named vector organisation names one languages. first item vector default language. names vector must match language code. email email address organisation. Used contact organisation. used detect person affiliated organisation. orcid Whether organisation requires ORCID every person uses organisation affiliation. rightsholder required copyright holder status organisation. "optional" means organisation required copyright holder. "single" means organisation must copyright holder. "shared" means organisation must one copyright holders. "" means copyright holder specified, organisation must copyright holder. funder required funder status organisation. categories rightsholder. license list allowed licenses organisation. list may contain following items: package, project, data. Every item must named character vector allowed licenses. names must match license name. values must either match path license template checklist package absolute URL publicly available markdown file license text. Use character(0) indicate organisation require specific license item. package defaults c("GPL-3.0", "MIT"). project defaults "CC 4.0". data defaults "CC0 1.0". ror optional ROR ID organisation. zenodo optional Zenodo community ID organisation. website optional website URL organisation. logo optional logo URL organisation.

what: Get the default organisation list from a git URL This function retrieves the default organisation list from a git URL. It checks if the organisation list is already cached in the user's R configuration. If it is, it returns the cached version. If not, it attempts to retrieve the organisation list from the specified git URL and caches it for future use. — org_list_from_url

title: Validate ROR — validate_ror
text: Research Organization Registry (ROR) global, community-led registry open persistent identifiers research organizations. ROR makes easy anyone system disambiguate institution names connect research organizations researchers research outputs. Validate ROR string 9 characters starting 0, followed 6 characters can letter (except , l, o) digit, ending 2 digits.

title: citeme 0.1.0
text: Migrate citation_meta class checklist. Export set interactive input functions: ask_email(), ask_language(), ask_new_license(), ask_orcid(), ask_ror(), ask_url(), ask_yes_no(), new_org_list(), new_org_item(), select_license(). Export set validation functions: validate_citation(), validate_language(), validate_license(), validate_orcid(), validate_ror(), validate_url(). Export organisation related functions: cache_org(), get_available_organisations() Export utility functions coalesce(), is_tracked_not_modified(), ssh_http(), org_list_from_url(). Rename *author author* functions *individual individual* functions. Add Markdown version licenses. Add checklist machinery. Add draft AI agent instructions. Remove dependencies fs sessioninfo packages.

title: citeme 0.0.1
text: Initial release Extracted person organisation management functionality checklist package. Core R6 classes: org_item org_list Author management functions: use_author(), store_authors(), author2df() Validation functions: validate_email(), validate_orcid() Support multiple languages, ORCID/ROR identifiers, licensing requirements.
